sanskrit: असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः । वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः ॥
hindiTranslation: जिसका मन पूरा वशमें नहीं है, उसके द्वारा योग प्राप्त होना कठिन है ।  परन्तु उपायपूर्वक यत्न करनेवाले वश्यात्माको योग प्राप्त हो सकता है, ऐसा मेरा मत है ॥
englishTranslation: Yoga is hard to attain for one whose mind is not fully controlled. But for one who controls the mind and strives with the right method, it can be reached.
essence: Yoga becomes reachable when the mind is trained, not merely hoped for.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person ruled by an uncontrolled mind struggles to reach yoga. With self-control, steady effort, and the right method, yoga becomes possible.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing the path is blocked by chaos, not by your worth.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna has already admitted that the mind is hard to control. Krishna answers with a practical promise: yoga is difficult for the undisciplined, but possible for the one who steadies the mind and keeps practicing.
modernRelevance: You sit at the edge of a hard conversation, rehearsing and retreating at once. The problem is not the conversation itself; it is the mind that keeps slipping away from your intention.
translationLiteral: असंयतात्मना (by one whose self is not controlled) / योगः (yoga) / दुष्प्रापः (hard to attain) / इति (thus) / मे (my) / मतिः (view) । / वश्यात्मना (by one whose self is under control) / तु (but) / यतता (by one striving) / शक्यः (possible) / अवाप्तुम् (to attain) / उपायतः (by right means)
